title: Stenoloma virescens
taxonomy: Stenoloma virescens belongs to the family Aspleniaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns. This classification places Stenoloma virescens within the larger group of vascular plants, which also includes conifers, cycads, and other types of plants. As a member of the Aspleniaceae family, Stenoloma virescens shares characteristics with other ferns in this group, such as the presence of sporangia and the ability to reproduce via spores. Stenoloma virescens is also classified as a type of lycopodiophyte, which is a group of plants that belong to the division Pteridophyta. The exact classification of Stenoloma virescens can be further refined at the species and genus levels, where it is often classified within the genus Stenoloma.

intro: Stenoloma virescens, also known as the green-rimmed spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. The health benefits of Stenoloma virescens include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stenoloma virescens is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often incorporated into teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health-promoting effects.
